chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ شَهِدَ ثُمَّ رَجَعَ عَنْ شَهَادَتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 7 – The one who testifies, then retract from his testimony

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/227)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/227)
text: شَاهِدِ الزُّورِ مَا تَوْبَتُهُ قَالَ يُؤَدِّي مِنَ الْمَالِ الَّذِي شَهِدَ عَلَيْهِ بِقَدْرِ مَا ذَهَبَ مِنْ مَالِهِ إِنْ كَانَ النِّصْفَ أَوِ الثُّلُثَ إِنْ كَانَ شَهِدَ هَذَا وَ آخَرُ مَعَهُ .

en.hubeali: Abu Ali Al Ashary, from Muhammad Bin Abdul Jabbar, from Safwan, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> regarding the false testifier, what is his repentance?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘He would pay from his wealth to the one whom he had testified against by the measurement of what had gone from his wealth, whether it was the half, or the third, if this one had testified and another one was with him’.
en.sarwar: 2. Abu Ali al-Ash ‘ariy has narrated from Muhammad ibn ‘Abd al-Jabbar from Safwan from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who testifies falsely and upon his repenting, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, has said, ‘He must pay for the assets that are gone due to his false testimony, like one-half or one-third if another one has testified with him.’”
